pipeline_models._rationale: Cross-model critique. The drafter (Claude Sonnet) and the critic (GPT-5.5) are different models from different vendors. When they agree something is wrong, the signal is much stronger than one model self-critiquing. The decider (Claude Sonnet again) synthesizes the two perspectives. Same pattern gstack uses for /codex review of Claude-authored work.

series_anchor.label: Sanskrit smṛti corpus (Manu, Yājñavalkya, Nārada, Bṛhaspati, Aṅgirasa, Parāśara, Viṣṇu, Yama, Kātyāyana, Vīramitrodaya)
series_anchor.palette: sandalwood, sacred saffron, deep ochre, weathered bronze, palm-leaf cream
series_anchor.mood: Ancient Indian dharma tradition committed to palm leaf. Pre-dawn temple light. The verse-numbered shloka, copied for centuries.
series_anchor.watercolor_treatment: Sandalwood wash as the dominant pigment, allowed to bleed into the paper. Saffron introduced sparingly. A touch of dried-leaf brown for the manuscript edge. Pigment kept thin and allowed to expose the cream paper warmly.

1_context.content_excerpt:
To Yama, residing in his hermitage, seated comfortably, a master of the Vedas and Śāstras, / the sages approached and asked, their minds held in check. // YSS_1.1 //

The Sages:
Those who have committed great sins, and minor sinners as well, / by which vows they are purified—tell that to us, O great sage. // YSS_1.2 //

Yama:
Touched by the wind and sun during the day, by the stars and breezes at night, / or by the two twilights, water is always purifying. // YSS_1.3 //

In its natural state, not pervaded by any impure substance (*amedhya*), it is always pure; / whether in a vessel or on the g

output.reasoning: Yama Smṛti opens with sages approaching a hermitage to ask about purification—the first answer given is water as a purifying element, touched by wind, sun, stars, and twilight. A single clay water vessel evokes both the dharmic ritual setting and the central metaphor of purification without illustrating any figure or scene.
output.subject: A single clay water vessel, slightly worn, resting on a flat surface
output.palette: Raw umber dominant, yellow ochre supporting, a sparse touch of burnt sienna accent
output.composition: Vessel placed in the lower-left third of the composition, tilting very slightly, with the upper-right two-thirds of the painting left as open cream paper breathing room; visual weight anchors the eye downward and left
output.watercolor_treatment: Sandalwood-toned raw umber laid in wet-on-wet, pooling darker at the base of the vessel where it meets the surface and diluting upward into near-nothing at the rim; yellow ochre introduced while still damp at the widest belly of the pot, bleeding softly outward; a dry-brush scuff of burnt sienna along one edge where the brush ran lean; cold-press grain visible through the thinned upper wash; pencil under-drawing faintly legible at the lip and handle; no second wash except a single deepening glaze at the shadow beneath the base
output.prompt: A single ancient clay water vessel rests in the lower-left third of warm cream cotton paper, its form built from a wet-on-wet sandalwood wash of raw umber that pools darkest at the base and thins upward until the paper grain shows through almost bare. Yellow ochre is introduced while the first wash is still damp, bleeding softly into the belly of the pot without a hard edge. A lean dry-brush stroke of burnt sienna traces one side where the brush ran nearly dry, leaving the cold-press texture exposed. A faint pencil under-drawing is barely legible at the rim. One deepening glaze of raw umber settles beneath the vessel as a soft shadow. The upper-right two-thirds of the composition is untouched cream paper, open and breathing.

output.reasoning: The critic found no issues and recommends shipping as-is. The original prompt is strong, honors the series anchor, and stays within the negative directives. No suggestions to accept or reject. The final prompt is the original, unchanged.
output.final_prompt: A single ancient clay water vessel rests in the lower-left third of warm cream cotton paper, its form built from a wet-on-wet sandalwood wash of raw umber that pools darkest at the base and thins upward until the paper grain shows through almost bare. Yellow ochre is introduced while the first wash is still damp, bleeding softly into the belly of the pot without a hard edge. A lean dry-brush stroke of burnt sienna traces one side where the brush ran nearly dry, leaving the cold-press texture exposed. A faint pencil under-drawing is barely legible at the rim. One deepening glaze of raw umber settles beneath the vessel as a soft shadow. The upper-right two-thirds of the composition is untouched cream paper, open and breathing.
output.changes_summary: No changes were made. The critic recommended shipping the original prompt as-is with no issues identified.
